title: [phase:critique] [family:audit-hardening] [provider_hint:codex] Current task: openclaw-tmux replacement. Critique the prototype's audit design. Return top 5 hardening steps to move from append-only JSONL + fsync toward stronger tamper evidence without overbuilding v1. No edits.
summary_text: - **Hint rule requirement:** `resolve-orchestration-context` only emits NotebookLM content hints when issue title/body contains `notebooklm|notebook lm|nlm`, plus: - `slide-prep` pattern for `notebooklm-slide-prep`, or - `mind map/infographic/図式化` pattern for `notebooklm-visual-brief`. - **Current repo reality:** direct paginated scan of all issues (`state=all`) found **no issue** matching those NotebookLM trigger tokens. - **Result:** there is **no safe existing open issue_number** to satisfy your requirement without editing issue/comment content. - **Safest path (fail-closed):** - Treat runtime-smoke dispatch target as **BLOCKED** under current “no issue/comment edits” constraint. - Do **not** run `notebooklm-runtime-smoke.yml` yet (would skip NotebookLM runtime lane and give false completion signal). - **If one live smoke is mandatory anyway:** - Require an explicit exception to create one throwaway issue with NotebookLM trigger text, then dispatch once with `post_result_comment=false`, then close. - Without that exception, safest action is to stop at readiness evidence only.
